sections.title: Basic Search
sections.html:
To search, type your query into the search box and press Enter (or click the Search button).

Multiple words are combined with AND by default. For example, open source finds documents containing both "open" and "source".

sections.title: Search Operators
sections.html:
OperatorDescriptionExample
AND / &&Both terms must appearfess AND search
OR / ||Either term may appearfess OR opensearch
NOT / -Exclude the termfess -lucene
"..."Exact phrase"full text search"
site:Restrict to a sitesite:example.com fess
filetype:Restrict to a file typefiletype:pdf fess

sections.title: Wildcards
sections.html:
You can use * to match any sequence of characters and ? to match a single character.

Examples: fess* matches "fess", "fessbook", "fessful". te?t matches "test" or "text".

sections.title: Range Queries
sections.html: Use square brackets to search for a range of values: content_length:[1024 TO 10240] finds documents with sizes between 1KB and 10KB.

sections.title: Facet Filters
sections.html: Use the sidebar facets to narrow your search by label, time period, file size, or file type. Click a facet item to apply it, and click the chip at the top of the results to remove it.

sections.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
sections.html:
• / — Focus the search box
  • Esc — Close suggestions or modal
  • Enter — Run search or select suggestion
